alerts.title: Iran rejects imminent deal at Qatar talks, vows to fund more missiles and drones
alerts.summary: BBC reports Tehran says a deal with the US is 'not imminent' even as delegations gather in Doha. Iran's Foreign Ministry, relayed via ME Spectator (~41K views), stated unfrozen funds will 'definitely' be used to 'produce more missiles and drones' — signaling the Qatar track is hardening rather than de-escalating despite Trump's public peace messaging.

alerts.title: CENTCOM confirms fresh strikes on Iranian missile sites and mine-laying boats
alerts.summary: PBS, BBC, NYT, NPR and Guardian report a new wave of US 'self-defense' strikes across southern Iran (~32°N, 53°E) targeting missile launch sites and small boats attempting to emplace sea mines. CENTCOM's spokesman confirmed the action to Fox News, framing it as restraint under ceasefire — but the strikes coincide with the Doha talks and Iran-coast operations near the Strait of Hormuz.

alerts.title: Khamenei reportedly rules out transferring 60% enriched uranium out of Iran
alerts.summary: A Reuters exclusive cited by global_metrics says Supreme Leader Khamenei has told Iranian sources the country's enriched uranium stockpile 'must stay in Iran,' directly rejecting Trump's Truth Social ultimatum demanding the material be surrendered or destroyed in place under IAEA-equivalent supervision. Breakout estimate remains ~1–2 weeks at 60% U-235.

alerts.title: Iran-linked hacker group sending threatening messages directly to US troops
alerts.summary: Stars and Stripes (via global_metrics cyber headlines) reports US service members are receiving direct threatening messages from an Iran-linked hacker group, coinciding with reports of GPS jamming disrupting Middle East navigation and CENTCOM deploying a defensive 'umbrella' of tech assets around shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

alerts.title: NATO ARRC rehearses deep strikes into Russia in 'Arcade Strike' exercise
alerts.summary: DeepState UA (~218K views) reports NATO's UK-led Allied Rapid Reaction Corps stood up a wartime HQ on a disused Charing Cross Underground platform to wargame deep strikes into Russia in response to a simulated attack on a Baltic state. Exercise tested electronic warfare, comms suppression and counter-drone tasks — a notable signal of NATO posture as the Ukraine and Iran fronts run concurrently.

analysis_note: Despite Doha talks underway, both sides are hardening: Iran rules out surrendering uranium and pledges to rearm with unfrozen funds while CENTCOM continues striking southern Iran — keeping threat level at 9 and Brent near $96.
